channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Orbital current from phonons
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03139-6

Chiral phonons, quasiparticles of lattice vibrations arising from circular atomic motion, hold potential as carriers of angular momentum for next-generation technologies. Experiments show that they can generate orbital currents under thermal gradients.
item.dc:title: Orbital current from phonons
item.dc:creator: Takashi Kikkawa

item.title: Orbital Seebeck effect induced by chiral phonons
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 21 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03134-x

Generation of orbital currents in a non-magnetic material can be useful to build efficient orbitronic devices. Now, the interplay of chiral phonons and electrons is shown to produce orbital currents in α-quartz.
item.dc:title: Orbital Seebeck effect induced by chiral phonons
item.dc:creator: Yoji Nabei
Cong Yang
Hong Sun
Hana Jones
Thuc Mai
Tian Wang

Z. Valy Vardeny
Binghai Yan
Xiaosong Li

Jun Liu
Dali Sun

item.title: Strong correlations and superconductivity in the supermoiré lattice
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 20 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03131-0

When two moiré patterns interfere with each other, they produce a longer-wavelength supermoiré pattern. Now, the effects of a supermoiré lattice on the band structure and transport properties of twisted trilayer graphene is investigated.
item.dc:title: Strong correlations and superconductivity in the supermoiré lattice
item.dc:creator: Zekang Zhou
Cheng Shen
Kryštof Kolár^
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Cyprian Lewandowski
Mitali Banerjee

item.title: Driving Floquet physics with excitonic fields
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 19 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03132-z

Floquet engineering is often limited by weak light–matter coupling and heating. Now it is shown that exciton-driven fields in monolayer semiconductors produce stronger, longer-lived Floquet effects and reveal hybridization linked to excitonic phases.
item.dc:title: Driving Floquet physics with excitonic fields
item.dc:creator: Vivek Pareek
David R. Bacon
Xing Zhu
Yang-Hao Chan
Fabio Bussolotti
Marcos G. Menezes
Nicholas S. Chan
Joel Pérez Urquizo
Kenji Watanabe
Takashi Taniguchi
Enrico Perfetto
Michael K. L. Man
Julien Madéo
Gianluca Stefanucci
Diana Y. Qiu
Kuan Eng Johnson Goh
Felipe H. da Jornada
Keshav M. Dani

item.title: Tracing dynamic arrest
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03159-2

Suspensions of colloidal hard spheres are excellent model systems for studying glass dynamics. Adding tracer particles enables a hydrodynamic approach for probing the glass transition.
item.dc:title: Tracing dynamic arrest
item.dc:creator: Andreas Neophytou

item.title: Transport evidence for chiral surface states from three-dimensional Landau bands
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03146-7

The properties of electronic transport through edge states of three-dimensional quantum Hall-like states are not yet resolved. Now, increasing the surface area of the edges is shown to produce increased conductance, suggesting that chiral surface states are present.
item.dc:title: Transport evidence for chiral surface states from three-dimensional Landau bands
item.dc:creator: Junho Seo
Chunyu Mark Guo
Carsten Putzke
Xiangwei Huang
Berit H. Goodge
Yip Chun Wong
Mark H. Fischer
Titus Neupert
Philip J. W. Moll

item.title: Emergent signatures of the glass transition in colloidal suspensions
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03140-z

Colloidal suspensions are known to display a glass transition. Now, insights into this transition, via its effect on the solvent, are gained by probing the correlated motion of tracer particles in such systems.
item.dc:title: Emergent signatures of the glass transition in colloidal suspensions
item.dc:creator: Patrick Laermann
Haim Diamant
Yael Roichman
Ivo Buttinoni
Manuel A. Escobedo-Sánchez
Stefan U. Egelhaaf
